routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 6 whites & 3 yolks of beldi eggs fried with 5g of olive oil
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 55g + warm water
| — 1 scoop of protein powder with water
| — 1/2 small apple
| — 1/4 small peach
| — 1/2 small kiwi
| — 2 garlic cloves
-----------------------

14:00 — return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — kefta 90/10 284g raw cooked with 1 small onion
| — brown rice 12g raw
| — 300g legume sauté with 8g of olive oil (carrot, courgette, onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 perly nature
| — 2g Almonds
| — 2g Walnuts
| — 2g Cashews
| — 2g Pistachios
| — 1 kiwi
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: Rest-day carb target is ~130g. Bread (breakfast) + oat 55g + rice 70g would land carbs at 124% (161g), on the edge of the warn zone. Trimming rice by 25g — the lowest-fiber-density carb source in the meal — brings carbs to 110% (right at the good-range ceiling) and calories to almost exactly 1,800, without touching protein or fat.
Protocol's anti-gas 'do' rule calls for cumin in every meal — gas/bloating is the #1 flagged issue. Mehdi confirmed cumin was added at lunch; dinner still has none.

The post-breakfast apple, peach, and kiwi pushed carbs to 123% (warn). A further rice trim (45g→25g) removes ~14.5g carbs, bringing carbs down to 112% — not quite back to 'good' but much closer — while calories land almost exactly on the 1,800 target.

adjustments.to: Chicken breast 233g raw, skinless + brown rice 10g; legume sauté oil increased 5g→17g
adjustments.reason: Requested: rebuild lunch + dinner (breakfast untouched) so the full day lands at exactly 100% of every target. With breakfast fixed, the remaining protein/carb/fat budget for lunch+dinner was too tight for kefta's fat:protein ratio — hitting 90.7g protein without blowing the 32.8g fat budget only works with a much leaner protein. Chicken breast (2.6g fat/100g vs. kefta's 8g/100g) closes the exact gap; rice drops to 10g since the carb budget left after breakfast's bread/oat/fruit is only ~53g. Trade-off: B12 drops from 214% to 12.5% and iron/copper both fall out of the good range — kefta was carrying those, chicken doesn't replace them.

adjustments.to: Corrected — pumpkin seeds were not actually eaten at breakfast, removed
adjustments.reason: Mehdi confirmed the pumpkin seeds were never eaten. Removing them moves the day off the exact 100% fit computed with them included, but all three macros stay comfortably in the good range (95-99%).

adjustments.from: Lunch rebuilt around chicken breast 233g (to force an exact macro fit)
adjustments.to: Reverted to kefta 90/10 — kefta stays, quantity re-solved instead
adjustments.reason: Mehdi can't actually swap in chicken breast today. Kefta is restored and the remaining protein/carb/fat budget (given the corrected, pumpkin-seed-free breakfast) is re-solved around it: kefta 284g, rice 12g, legume sauté oil 8g. This lands kcal at 99.9%, protein 100%, carbs 100%, fat 102% — nearly as tight as the chicken version — while keeping B12, iron, and copper at kefta's normal (strong) levels instead of the chicken-driven collapse.

verdict.text_html: Kefta restored, still an almost exact fit — 1,799 kcal (99.9%), protein 100%, carbs 100%, fat 102% — and this time B12/iron/copper stay at kefta's normal strong levels instead of collapsing.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Kefta re-solved at 284g against the corrected breakfast — essentially exact

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Kefta 284g solves for exactly the protein still needed after the corrected breakfast

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Rice re-solved to 12g — the carb budget after bread, oat, and breakfast fruit is still tight

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Kefta's richer fat content means a smaller legume-sauté oil portion (8g) closes the gap exactly

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Legume sauté + oat + fruit carry this — rice at just 12g keeps this short

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Cumin was only at breakfast, now added to lunch too — dinner still has none; onion is cooked (not raw) in both dishes, which is fine per protocol

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner well before 22:00 ✓ — cumin now covered at breakfast + lunch, dinner still missing it

coverage.name: Vitamin C
coverage.note: Apple + peach + kiwi at breakfast pushed this well past the dinner kiwi + vegetables alone

coverage.name: Calcium
coverage.note: No milk (protocol dont) and only one perly today — weakest spot of the day

good: Kefta restored and the day still lands almost exactly on target — kcal 99.9%, protein 100%, carbs 100%, fat 102%
B12 (323%), Selenium (199%), and Choline (108%) all back to kefta's normal strong levels — the chicken-breast version's collapse is avoided entirely
Vitamin C strong at 162% — apple, peach, and kiwi at breakfast on top of the dinner kiwi
Cumin now added to both lunch and dinner, closing the gap flagged on every prior day
bad: Rice still very small at 12g to make the carbs fit exactly — a token portion, not a real side
Magnesium down to ~38% and Potassium ~46% — kefta at 284g plus a small rice/legume portion doesn't carry as much of either as a bigger rice/potato side would
Fiber still short at ~21g / 30g (70%) — no potato/banana today, and rice stays minimal
Calcium (16%), Iodine (13%), Chromium — structural gaps on a day with no dairy, fatty fish, or avocado
loggedItems: 6 egg whites + 3 egg yolks (beldi), fried with 5g olive oil
Kefta 90/10 284g raw, cooked with 1 small onion (~50g)
Brown rice 12g raw (re-solved from 70g to hit the exact carb budget with kefta restored)
300g légumes sautés (carrot 100g, courgette 100g, onion 100g) with 8g olive oil

fullTable.note: Kefta 284g + 2 whey scoops + eggs — exactly on target
3 × 1L blocks from the schedule
Oat, legumes, and the breakfast apple/peach/kiwi contribute, but rice down to 10g and no potato/banana keep this short
Beldi eggs' richer yolks + walnuts 2g — better than plain eggs but still low, no fatty fish
3 egg yolks only — structural gap without fatty fish
Kefta 284g + oat + rice — back to a healthy iron day now that kefta is restored
Kefta restored — comfortably the best-covered nutrient of the day again
No pumpkin seeds today and rice down to 12g — oat + kefta carry what's left, weakest magnesium day so far
No milk (protocol dont) — only one perly nature today, weakest spot of the day
Kefta 284g restores this comfortably back into the good range
Kefta + vegetables + kiwi + perly + the breakfast fruit, but no potato/banana today
Oat, rice, vegetables, kiwi, yolks, and the breakfast fruit each contribute a little
Apple + peach + kiwi at breakfast on top of the dinner kiwi + vegetables push this well past target
Carrot + beldi egg yolks (richer in Vit A than standard eggs)
Structural gap — no dairy, seafood, or iodized salt today
3 olive oil portions + almonds, but no avocado today — lower than usual
Kiwi (breakfast + dinner) + carrot + courgette — no avocado today to help
Kefta + garlic + breakfast fruit push this comfortably past target
Kefta + eggs + garlic comfortably cover this
Oat and bread carry most of this — rice at just 12g adds only a little
Eggs (whites + yolks) and kefta cover this well
Kefta 284g dominant, bread adds a little more
Kefta 284g + nuts — back to the normal warn-range level now that kefta is restored
3 egg yolks + kefta 284g land comfortably past target again
Fromage frais mild — consider swapping for raib for better gut value
Bread + kefta — comfortably within normal range
Carrot, courgette, kiwi, apple, peach, olive oil, nuts — the breakfast fruit broadened this from earlier
Eggs + kefta + perly comfortably clear the target
Oat + garlic carry most of this — rice at just 12g contributes only a little
Oat and rice are the only grain sources — lower than days with potato/banana
Egg yolks + kefta cover this comfortably
3 egg yolks provide the bulk of this
